issue.errorLine1: La aplicación infringe la Politica W^X y es capaz de escribir y ejecutar en el mismo directorio o en la misma parte de memoria. Esto permite la ejecución de ejecutables arbitrarios, ya sea modificando los ejecutables integrados en la aplicación o descargándolos de Internet. A menos que este sea el comportamiento previsto de la aplicación (p. ej., emuladores de terminal), se recomienda buscar una versión más reciente de la aplicación que apunte al SDK 29 (Android 10) y versiones posteriores, o buscar alternativas.

<string name="disclaimer_body">AppManagerNG exposes privileged operations that can break apps or your device when misused. The maintainers accept no responsibility for damage caused by using this application. If you are not yet comfortable with what root, Shizuku, or ADB privileges actually do, stay on the no-root path until you are.

Any link to a third-party app or website is provided for convenience only. The maintainers do not control or vouch for that external content.

By continuing, you accept full responsibility for how you use AppManagerNG and waive any claim for damages or monetary compensation arising from misuse.</string>

issue.errorLine1:
<string name="help_appops_body">App ops are a parallel permission system Android tracks behind the visible permission list. Each op has four modes — ALLOWED, IGNORED (silently denies the call), ERRORED (the app crashes if it asks), and DEFAULT (uses the platform default).

Where this matters:
• Apps that ignore permission revokes via reflection — toggling the matching app op forces the silent deny.
• MIUI / HyperOS extends the op range to 10001-10039 with vendor-specific ops; AppManagerNG surfaces these with readable labels (Autostart, Background launch activity, Display while screen locked, etc.).
• AppManagerNG's App Details -> App ops tab cycles ALLOWED -> IGNORED -> ERRORED -> ALLOWED on tap; long-press opens the full picker with FOREGROUND and other modes.</string>

issue.message: Replace "-" with an "en dash" character (–, –) ?
issue.errorLine1:
<string name="help_trackers_body">Trackers are third-party SDKs that report analytics or advertising data back to a vendor. AppManagerNG cross-references each app's classes against the εxodus dataset bundled in the APK (or freshly fetched if you opt in to debloat-definition updates).

What the badge counts mean:
• Green chip (low): a few or no trackers.
• Amber chip: 5-19 components matched.
• Red chip: 20+ components matched.

The "organizations" count rolls up multiple SDKs from the same vendor — e.g. Google's AdMob + Analytics + Crashlytics + Firebase show as 4 components but 1 organization.</string>
